attributes.description: AWS ECS dashboard for CloudWatch metrics collected by the OpenTelemetry Collector.
attributes.title: [AWS OTel] ECS

embeddableConfig.description:
Monitor your AWS ECS / ECS services in real time.
Pick services via the top-bar selector, or click any row in the ECS services table to filter the dashboard by service / cluster / region.
State badges follow the same palette as the Services-by-state donut (red=saturated, amber=hot, green=healthy, grey=idle).
Total number of ECS services — distinct ServiceName values in the latest collected data for the selected time range.

attributes.description: Total number of ECS services — distinct ServiceName values in the latest collected data for the selected time range.

query.esql: FROM metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" | STATS last_seen = MAX(@timestamp) BY ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.region | INLINE STATS latest = MAX(last_seen) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest) <= 5 | STATS total = COUNT(*) | KEEP total

FROM metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" | STATS last_seen = MAX(@timestamp) BY ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.region | INLINE STATS latest = MAX(last_seen) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest) <= 5 | STATS total = COUNT(*) | KEEP total

embeddableConfig.title: Services by state
embeddableConfig.description: Share of ECS services by latest state — saturated (CPU or memory > 80%), hot (> 50%), idle (CPU < 5%), or healthy. Uses the most recent value per service within a 5-minute freshness window. A growing saturated slice indicates services under resource pressure.

attributes.title: Services by state
attributes.description: Share of ECS services by latest state — saturated (CPU or memory > 80%), hot (> 50%), idle (CPU < 5%), or healthy. Uses the most recent value per service within a 5-minute freshness window. A growing saturated slice indicates services under resource pressure.

query.esql: FROM metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(@timestamp) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", @timestamp, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS cpu_cur = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL)), mem_cur = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL)) BY ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.region | EVAL state = CASE(cpu_cur IS NULL AND mem_cur IS NULL, "no data", cpu_cur > 80 OR mem_cur > 80, "saturated", cpu_cur > 50 OR mem_cur > 50, "hot", cpu_cur < 5, "idle", "healthy") | STATS services = COUNT(*) BY state

FROM metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(@timestamp) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", @timestamp, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS cpu_cur = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL)), mem_cur = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL)) BY ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.region | EVAL state = CASE(cpu_cur IS NULL AND mem_cur IS NULL, "no data", cpu_cur > 80 OR mem_cur > 80, "saturated", cpu_cur > 50 OR mem_cur > 50, "hot", cpu_cur < 5, "idle", "healthy") | STATS services = COUNT(*) BY state

embeddableConfig.title: Service count over time (by state)
embeddableConfig.description: Count of ECS services over time, classified per time bucket as saturated, hot, healthy, or idle by peak CPU/memory utilization. Distinct services are counted per bucket. A rising saturated series indicates spreading resource pressure.

attributes.title: Service count over time (by state)
attributes.description: Count of ECS services over time, classified per time bucket as saturated, hot, healthy, or idle by peak CPU/memory utilization. Distinct services are counted per bucket. A rising saturated series indicates spreading resource pressure.

query.esql: TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" | STATS cpu_max = MAX(max_over_time(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))), mem_max = MAX(max_over_time(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL))) BY timestamp = TBUCKET(50, ?_tstart, ?_tend), ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.region | EVAL state = CASE(cpu_max IS NULL AND mem_max IS NULL, "no data", cpu_max > 80 OR mem_max > 80, "saturated", cpu_max > 50 OR mem_max > 50, "hot", cpu_max < 5, "idle", "healthy") | STATS total = COUNT(*), saturated = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "saturated", hot = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "hot", healthy = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "healthy", idle = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "idle", no_data = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "no data" BY timestamp | SORT timestamp ASC

TS metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" | STATS cpu_max = MAX(max_over_time(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL))), mem_max = MAX(max_over_time(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL))) BY timestamp = TBUCKET(50, ?_tstart, ?_tend), ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.region | EVAL state = CASE(cpu_max IS NULL AND mem_max IS NULL, "no data", cpu_max > 80 OR mem_max > 80, "saturated", cpu_max > 50 OR mem_max > 50, "hot", cpu_max < 5, "idle", "healthy") | STATS total = COUNT(*), saturated = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "saturated", hot = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "hot", healthy = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "healthy", idle = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "idle", no_data = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "no data" BY timestamp | SORT timestamp ASC

embeddableConfig.title: ECS services
embeddableConfig.description: All ECS services, ranked worst-first by state. Click a service to drill into its metrics. State reflects the latest CPU/memory utilization within a 5-minute freshness window. Avg and Max CPU/memory are the average and peak utilization (%) over the selected time range; connection and processed-bytes columns are summed or averaged over the range.

query.esql: FROM metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" | STATS c_cpu_avg = AVG(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL)) / 100.0, c_cpu_max = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL)) / 100.0, c_mem_avg = AVG(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL)) / 100.0, c_mem_max = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL)) / 100.0, c_new_conn = SUM(CASE(stat == "Sum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/NewConnectionCount`, NULL)), c_active_conn = AVG(CASE(stat == "Sum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/ActiveConnectionCount`, NULL)), c_processed_bytes = SUM(CASE(stat == "Sum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/ProcessedBytes`, NULL)) BY ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.account.id, cloud.region | EVAL c_state = CASE(c_cpu_max IS NULL AND c_mem_max IS NULL, "no data", c_cpu_max > 0.8 OR c_mem_max > 0.8, "saturated", c_cpu_max > 0.5 OR c_mem_max > 0.5, "hot", c_cpu_max < 0.05, "idle", "healthy"), c_state_sort = CASE(c_cpu_max IS NULL AND c_mem_max IS NULL, 3, c_cpu_max > 0.8 OR c_mem_max > 0.8, 1, c_cpu_max > 0.5 OR c_mem_max > 0.5, 2, c_cpu_max < 0.05, 5, 4) | SORT c_state_sort ASC

FROM metrics-aws.ecs.otel-* | WHERE ServiceName IS NOT NULL AND ServiceName != "" AND ClusterName IS NOT NULL AND ClusterName != "" | STATS c_cpu_avg = AVG(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL)) / 100.0, c_cpu_max = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/CPUUtilization`, NULL)) / 100.0, c_mem_avg = AVG(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL)) / 100.0, c_mem_max = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/MemoryUtilization`, NULL)) / 100.0, c_new_conn = SUM(CASE(stat == "Sum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/NewConnectionCount`, NULL)), c_active_conn = AVG(CASE(stat == "Sum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/ActiveConnectionCount`, NULL)), c_processed_bytes = SUM(CASE(stat == "Sum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/ECS/ProcessedBytes`, NULL)) BY ServiceName, ClusterName, cloud.account.id, cloud.region | EVAL c_state = CASE(c_cpu_max IS NULL AND c_mem_max IS NULL, "no data", c_cpu_max > 0.8 OR c_mem_max > 0.8, "saturated", c_cpu_max > 0.5 OR c_mem_max > 0.5, "hot", c_cpu_max < 0.05, "idle", "healthy"), c_state_sort = CASE(c_cpu_max IS NULL AND c_mem_max IS NULL, 3, c_cpu_max > 0.8 OR c_mem_max > 0.8, 1, c_cpu_max > 0.5 OR c_mem_max > 0.5, 2, c_cpu_max < 0.05, 5, 4) | SORT c_state_sort ASC

embeddableConfig.title: ECS services — hexagon map (click an hex for actions)
embeddableConfig.description: One hexagon per ECS service, ordered worst-first with CPU- or memory-saturated services first. Toggle Color by between Status, CPU utilization, or Memory utilization; metric views support Min, Max, Avg, and Last over the dashboard time range. Click any hexagon to filter the dashboard or open metrics for that service.
